summary.en: Console gaming as a 2-sided platform: gamers (subsidy side, low-margin or loss-leader hardware) + game developers (money side, paying licensing fees per copy sold). Released November 2006, the Wii sold 101 million units lifetime — a top-3 best-selling console of all time — by deliberately serving the casual / family audience that PlayStation and Xbox were ignoring. BMG's second paradigm case for the Multi-Sided Platforms pattern, paired with Google. The contrast with Sony's PS3 (released the same month, sold at a loss with cutting-edge hardware) shows the platform-economics choice clearly: Nintendo bet on motion controls + a broader gamer side at lower hardware cost, then funded the platform via developer royalties.
summary.zh: 把游戏主机看作双边平台:玩家(补贴方,主机低毛利甚至亏损出货)+ 游戏开发者(收钱方,按销量付授权费)。2006 年 11 月发售,Wii 累计销量 1.01 亿台 —— 史上销量前三的主机之一 —— 靠的是有意服务 PlayStation 和 Xbox 一直忽视的「轻度玩家 / 家庭用户」。这是《商业模式新生代》中多边平台模式的第二个范式案例,与 Google 并列。Wii 与索尼同月发售的 PS3(顶级硬件、亏本出货)形成清晰对照,把平台经济学的取舍呈现得很清楚:任天堂赌「体感操作 + 在更低硬件成本下覆盖更广的玩家」,然后靠开发者版税养活整个平台。
